summary: Iteration 165: Verified GET redirect endpoints fix for Stripe 'Server error' issue. All GET redirect endpoints return 302 to Stripe (checkout.stripe.com and connect.stripe.com). Original POST endpoints still work for backward compatibility. Frontend correctly uses window.location.href for navigation.

wallet_page.details: Wallet page loads with Stripe Connect button and membership section

subscriptions_page.details: Subscriptions page shows all 4 tiers with upgrade buttons

server_error_fix.problem: 'Server error. Please try again in a moment.' on wallet/subscriptions buttons
server_error_fix.root_cause: CDN/proxy corrupts POST response JSON bodies, causing parse failures
server_error_fix.solution: GET redirect endpoints that return HTTP 302 - browser follows redirects natively, no JSON parsing needed

critical_code_review_comments: GET redirect pattern correctly bypasses JSON body parsing issues
Token passed as query parameter instead of Authorization header for GET requests
Backend properly validates token before creating Stripe sessions
Original POST endpoints preserved for backward compatibility
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 165 verified GET redirect endpoints fix. The key change is using GET endpoints that return 302 redirects to Stripe URLs instead of POST endpoints that return JSON. The frontend uses window.location.href to navigate, so the browser handles the redirect natively without any JSON parsing. All subscription tiers (bronze, silver, gold, diamond) and Stripe Connect onboarding work correctly.
